{{Short description|2002 film festival in Berlin, Germany}} {{Infobox Film festival | name = 52nd Berlin International Film Festival | image = 52nd Berlin International Film Festival poster.jpg | caption = Festival poster | opening = ''[[Heaven (2002 film)|Heaven]]'' | closing = ''[[The Great Dictator]]'' | location = [[Berlin]], [[Germany]] | founded = 1951 | awards = [[Golden Bear]]:
''[[Bloody Sunday (film)|Bloody Sunday]]''
''[[Spirited Away]]'' | host = | number = 389 films | date = 6–17 February 2002 | website = http://www.berlinale.de | chronology = Berlin International Film Festival | preceded_by = [[51st Berlin International Film Festival|51st]] | followed_by = [[53rd Berlin International Film Festival|53rd]] }} The '''52nd''' annual '''[[Berlin International Film Festival]]''' was held from February 6 to 17, 2002.{{cite web |url=http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2002/01_jahresblatt_2002/01_Jahresblatt_2002.html|title=52nd Berlin International Film Festival |access-date=2012-10-22 |work=berlinale.de}} The festival opened with ''[[Heaven (2002 film)|Heaven]]'' by [[Tom Tykwer]].{{cite web|url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2002/08_pressemitteilungen_2002/08_Pressemitteilungen_2002-Details_1108.html |title=BERLINALE 2002 OPENS WITH THE WORLD PREMIERE OF HEAVEN |access-date=1 September 2014 |work=berlinale.de | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091210050828/http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2002/08_pressemitteilungen_2002/08_Pressemitteilungen_2002-Details_1108.html| archive-date=10 December 2009 | url-status= live}}{{cite web|url=http://www.dw.de/chasing-a-golden-bear/a-436951 |title=Chasing a Golden Bear |access-date=1 September 2014 |work=dw.de | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140902212255/http://www.dw.de/chasing-a-golden-bear/a-436951| archive-date=2 September 2014 | url-status= live}} The new print of [[Charlie Chaplin]]'s 1940 American satirical [[dramedy]] film ''[[The Great Dictator]]'' was the closing film of the festival.{{cite web|url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2002/08_pressemitteilungen_2002/08_Pressemitteilungen_2002-Details_1067.html |title=18 WORLD PREMIERES AND 3 DIRECTING DEBUTS IN THE BERLINALE COMPETITION 2002 |access-date=1 September 2014 |work=berlinale.de | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140306192441/http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2002/08_pressemitteilungen_2002/08_Pressemitteilungen_2002-Details_1067.html| archive-date=6 March 2014 | url-status= live}} The [[Golden Bear]] was jointly awarded to ''[[Bloody Sunday (film)|Bloody Sunday]]'', directed by [[Paul Greengrass]], and ''[[Spirited Away]]'', by [[Hayao Miyazaki]]. The Retrospective section was dedicated to European films from the 1960s.{{cite web|url=http://www.berlinale.de/en/das_festival/sektionen_sonderveranstaltungen/retrospektive/index.html#|title=RETROSPECTIVE|access-date=28 August 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170304113847/http://www.berlinale.de/en/das_festival/sektionen_sonderveranstaltungen/retrospektive/index.html|archive-date=4 March 2017|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}} [[Dieter Kosslick]] became the director of the festival, taking over from [[Moritz de Hadeln]].{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2012/film/news/berlinale-beginnings-1118049905/|title=Berlinale beginnings |date=8 February 2012 |access-date=28 August 2014}} ==Juries== === Main Competition === The following people were announced as being on the jury for the festival:{{cite web|url=http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2002/04_jury_2002/04_Jury_2002.html |title=JURIES 2002
